claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 2 children:
  - Alex is 6 years old and lives with you.
  - Blake is 3 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 6 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

Regarding Blake:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 3 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child in hospital/residential accommodation for 13 weeks (>12) and claimant is not regularly spending money on child
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. To assess your eligibility, I need to ask you a few questions.

First, do you have any children that you're responsible for?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I do! I have two children - Alex who is 6 years old, and Blake who is 3 years old.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information! It's great that you have Alex (6 years old) and Blake (3 years old).

Now, do you currently live in the UK with Alex and Blake?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I live in the UK and both Alex and Blake live with me at home.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Perfect! Since both Alex and Blake are under 16 and live with you in the UK, that's a good start.

Do you have the right to reside in the UK? For example, are you a UK citizen, or do you have settled status, pre-settled status, or another immigration status?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I'm a UK citizen, so I have the right to reside here.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Excellent! Just a couple more questions to ensure I have all the information I need.

Is anyone else currently claiming Child Benefit for Alex or Blake?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for either of them.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for confirming that. One final question:

Are Alex and Blake currently in hospital, care, or any other living arrangement away from home for an extended period?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Well, Alex is fine and at home with me. But Blake has actually been in hospital for a few months now - about 3 months or so.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for sharing that information. I understand Blake has been in hospital for about 3 months.

A few follow-up questions about Blake's situation:

1. Is Blake in a UK hospital?
2. Are you regularly spending money on Blake while they're in hospital (for example, on clothes, toys, treats, or other items for them)?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, Blake is in a UK hospital. And no, I haven't been able to afford to spend money on their needs while they're in there.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Based on the information you've provided, here's my assessment of your Child Benefit eligibility:

**For Alex (6 years old): You ARE eligible ✓**
Alex is under 16, lives with you at home in the UK, you're a UK citizen, and no one else is claiming for Alex. You meet all the standard criteria for Child Benefit for Alex.

**For Blake (3 years old): You are NOT eligible ✗**
Unfortunately, because Blake has been in hospital for approximately 3 months (around 12 weeks), your eligibility for Child Benefit for Blake is affected. Under the rules, if a child is in hospital for more than 12 weeks, you usually stop being entitled to Child Benefit unless you're regularly spending money on them while they're in hospital. Since you've indicated you haven't been able to afford to spend money on Blake's needs during their hospital stay, you wouldn't be eligible for Child Benefit for Blake at this time.

**What you can do:**
- You can still claim Child Benefit for Alex
- If your circumstances change and you're able to start regularly spending money on Blake while they're in hospital, you should contact HMRC as this could affect your eligibility
- Once Blake comes home from hospital, you would be able to claim Child Benefit for them again

I hope Blake recovers soon and is able to come home.

reasonings: Alex is 6 years old (under 16), lives with the claimant in the UK, the claimant is a UK citizen with the right to reside, and no one else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex. Alex meets all the standard eligibility criteria for Child Benefit.

Blake is 3 years old and has been in a UK hospital for approximately 3 months (12 weeks). According to the rules, Child Benefit payments might be affected if a child goes into hospital for more than 12 weeks. After 12 weeks, the claimant is usually no longer entitled to Child Benefit unless they regularly spend money on the child. The claimant has confirmed they have not been able to afford to spend money on Blake's needs while in hospital. Since Blake has been in hospital for about 3 months (approximately 12 weeks) and the claimant is not regularly spending money on Blake, the claimant is not eligible for Child Benefit for Blake.
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant is a UK citizen living in the UK with two children: Alex (6) and Blake (3). No one else is claiming Child Benefit for either child. Alex lives at home and meets all standard eligibility criteria. However, Blake has been in a UK hospital for approximately 3 months (12 weeks). Under Child Benefit rules, after 12 weeks in hospital, entitlement usually ends unless the claimant regularly spends money on the child. Since the claimant is not regularly spending money on Blake, they are not eligible for Child Benefit for Blake, but they are eligible for Alex.
